commit.message:
Fix: tap existing items in text/shape mode to select instead of creating new

- Added hit-test in _onCanvasTap: tapping an existing item selects it
  instead of creating a new item on top
- Newly created text items and sticky notes auto-select to show format bar
- Format bar now reliably appears when interacting with any text-containing item

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement skill persistence — forged skills survive restarts

The self-evolution loop now accumulates knowledge across process restarts.
Previously every forged skill was lost when the server stopped; re-running
produced the same learning loop indefinitely with nothing to show for it.

What was built:

SkillRow DB table (models.py)
- New forged_skills table: name (PK), record_json, source_code,
  created_at, updated_at
- source_code stores the raw Python synthesized by SkillSynthesizer
- On reload the code is recompiled through _compile_extract and
  re-registered in the SkillRegistry — identical to the original forge path

SkillStore (storage/skill_store.py)
- save(record, source_code) — upsert with SQLite ON CONFLICT
- load_all() — returns PersistedSkill(record, source_code) ordered by
  created_at; silently skips corrupted or schema-incompatible rows
- update_status(name, status) — persists DEGRADED and other transitions
- update_shadow(name, rate, comparisons) — persists shadow eval results
- delete(name) — for manual cleanup

SkillForge (forge/forge.py)
- New skill_store parameter (optional, None = in-memory only, backward compat)
- After successfully registering a skill in memory, calls skill_store.save()
- Persist failure is non-fatal: skill lives in memory, will re-forge on
  the next evolution cycle if the process restarts before next persist

EvolutionLoop (loop.py)
- New skill_store parameter
- _shadow_evaluate() now calls skill_store.update_shadow() and
  update_status() after each evaluation — shadow metrics and DEGRADED
  status survive restarts

bootstrap.py
- skill_store wired into SkillForge and EvolutionLoop
- _reload_forged_skills() helper: loads all DB rows, recompiles each
  source_code, registers in registry; built-ins loaded first always win
  on name collision; corrupted rows skipped with a warning log
- EvoSysRuntime exposes skill_store field

Tests: 528 → 547 (19 new)
- test_skill_store.py: 17 tests covering save/load/upsert/update/delete
  and the full recompile→invoke roundtrip
- test_skill_persistence.py: 6 integration tests covering forge→save,
  reload→works, builtin precedence, corrupted code graceful skip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add mobile touch controls, fullscreen button, and iOS Safari audio fix

Virtual joystick (left) and fire button (right) appear on touch devices
during gameplay, injecting synthetic input via Input.action_press/release
so player.gd needs zero changes. Multi-touch supported via finger index
tracking. Menu screens accept tap input (tap to start, retry, skip, etc.).

Fullscreen toggle button (expand/collapse icon, top-right) persists across
all screens on touch devices via main.gd.

iOS Safari audio fix: deploy workflow injects a script that patches the
AudioContext constructor to track and resume all instances on first user
gesture. Title music is also deferred on first load to avoid playing
before any interaction.

Phase 33: iptables-save Import

Add iptables-import subcommand that converts Linux iptables-save output
into PacGate YAML rules. Establishes quad input format (YAML + P4 +
Wireshark + iptables).

Features:
- Line-based iptables-save parser with shell-style tokenizer
- Protocol/port/CIDR/TCP-flags/ICMP/conntrack-state/MAC mapping
- Multiport expansion (--dports 80,443 → separate rules)
- DNAT/SNAT → RewriteAction extraction
- Chain selection (--chain INPUT/FORWARD/OUTPUT/all)
- JSON summary output (--json flag)

Tests: 685 unit + 410 integration = 1095 Rust tests (all passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
PageSpeed optimization: remove 100+ fonts, Wikipedia images, heavy animations

- Reduce Google Fonts from ~100 to 7 essential (Montserrat, Noto Sans, Bowlby One SC, Russo One, Handjet, Press Start 2P, Gveret Levin)
- Remove TFMix variable font @font-face and letter-breathe animation
- Replace complex logo (15+ keyframes, font cycling, per-letter animations) with lightweight 3D CSS perspective effect using GPU-composited transforms
- Remove Wikipedia portrait images (38 URLs + eager preload + cycling JS)
- Remove 110-font cycling JS (LOGO_FONTS array, CASE_PATTERNS, startFontCycle)
- Simplify .logo-dot-tv: remove tvNeon/tvFlicker/tvGlitch animations, use static glow
- Simplify screen-logo-jaffa: remove miniStripeScroll infinite animation
- New logo JS auto-stops when TV starts (no CPU waste during video playback)
- Net reduction: -172 lines, ~258 lines of dead code removed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use project venv python in MCP server registration

The setup script was registering the MCP server with "command": "python3",
which resolves to whatever Python is on PATH at runtime. On machines where
google-api-python-client is only installed in the project venv (not system-
wide), this caused "No module named 'google'" errors in every VSCode project.

Fix: create a dedicated venv/ during setup and register the MCP server with
the absolute path to the venv's Python binary. This guarantees the server
always runs with the Python that has its dependencies installed, regardless
of the system Python or any project-specific venv.

README updated to document the venv approach and explain why the venv Python
path is used instead of the system python3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix off-by-one append bug in Section B and D of strategy tabs

Previously, _get_all_values read from the header row (_SEC_B_ROW+1),
so nav_data[i] mapped to sheet row _SEC_B_ROW+1+i but the formula
used _SEC_B_ROW+2+i, causing every run to find the same "first empty"
slot and overwrite instead of append.

Fix: read from _SEC_B_ROW+2 / _SEC_D_ROW+2 (skipping the header)
so nav_data[i] == sheet row _SEC_B_ROW+2+i and the formula is correct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix parallel file corruption by grouping mutations per file (#4)

* Fix parallel file corruption by grouping mutations per file

When running with multiple workers, the Pool round-robin distributed
mutations across workers regardless of which file they targeted. This
caused multiple workers to write mutated source to the same file
simultaneously, corrupting it (Bus Error, empty files).

Now partitions mutations by file_path so all mutations for a given file
are handled by the same worker. Uses largest-first bin packing for
balanced distribution across workers.

Also adds CI timeout (5min) to prevent CI hangs from fork-based tests.

commit.message:
feat: Standardize GitHub Issues & Consolidated Backlog (#260)

* docs(plan): add implementation plan for GitHub issue standardization and consolidated backlog

Covers ingestion of external tracker issues during adoption, field normalization
to canonical backlog schema, consolidated CLI/UI view across all sources, and
flag-gated auto-import triggered by process.intake.autoImport.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* feat(intake): implement GitHub issue import, consolidated backlog, and auto-import on adoption

Add runtime infrastructure for standardizing external tracker issues into the
canonical backlog schema and surfacing a consolidated view across all sources.

New commands:
- `import-issues` — fetches from GitHub (via gh CLI), normalizes fields using
  configurable label/state/team maps, deduplicates against existing backlog,
  writes to both .claude/state/backlog.json and AGENT_BACKLOG.md
- `backlog` — consolidated viewer with --format (table/json/yaml/csv),
  --team, --priority, --source, --status filters
- `sync-backlog` — runtime handler combining external tracker + orchestrator
  state into orchestrated sync

Architecture:
- tracker-adapter.mjs — factory for tracker adapters
- github-adapter.mjs — GitHub adapter using gh CLI
- linear-adapter.mjs — Linear stub with clear interface
- issue-normalizer.mjs — field normalization, priority inference, team
  routing, acceptance criteria extraction, deduplication
- backlog-store.mjs — dual JSON + Markdown persistence with filter/sort

Adoption integration:
- process.intake.autoImport flag in project.yaml triggers automatic issue
  import during agentkit init (same pattern as evaluation.infraEval)
- Configurable importLabelsMap, importStateMap, importTeamMap

Validation:
- spec-validator validates import map values (priorities, statuses)
- Warns when autoImport=true but issueTracker=none
- 38 new tests (all 614 pass)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* fix(intake): address code review findings for issue import pipeline

- Fix includes() false positives in inferPriority/inferTeam (exact match only)
- Fix sync-backlog --team filter persisting filtered subset (now display-only)
- Add --state to sync-backlog VALID_FLAGS in CLI router
- Clamp --limit to max 1000 in GitHub adapter
- Fix CSV escape to handle newlines

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* docs(plan): address PR review comments on implementation plan

- Fix gaps table: sync-backlog was missing from CLI, not just handler
- Add csv to architecture diagram format list for consistency
- Clarify autoImport gates auto-run, not command availability
- Fix discovery source reference from AGENT_TEAMS.md to agentkit discover
- Fix success criteria: --priority takes single value, not comma list
- Add commands: top-level key to YAML snippet to match real structure

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* fix(intake): address PR review thread 3892715826

- Add importTeamMap cross-validation against teams.yaml in spec-validator
- Validate --since date parsing in github-adapter (reject invalid dates)
- Fall back to readBacklogMarkdown when JSON store is empty in both
  import-issues and sync-backlog-runner (prevents data loss on first run)
- Fix null externalId collisions in deduplicateItems (treat as manual)
- Update readBacklogMarkdown to parse both 7-column and 6-column formats
- Fix init.mjs error message to use pnpm invocation pattern
- Update plan status from DRAFT to IMPLEMENTED
- Add import-issues integration test suite (7 tests)

All 637 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* chore: regenerate synced outputs after intake feature changes

Re-ran agentkit sync to propagate updated template variables
(autoImport flag, import-issues command) to all platform configs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* docs(plan): address pr260 review thread feedback

* fix(docs): remove hard-coded test count from intake flow doc

Replace "All 614 tests pass" with a generic statement that won't
drift as tests are added or removed.

Addresses PR #260 review comment 11.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuVpmePLgho73rMidY9nat

* fix(intake): address PR review findings and code review issues

- Remove unused `join` import and `matchedLines` variable in backlog-store
- Fix 6-column regex to exclude 7-column rows (anchored end-of-line)
- Always pass --state to gh CLI (fixes --state all not working)
- Add date validation for --since flag (already present, verified)
- Fix preserved count in deduplicateItems to exclude appended incoming items
- Add deterministic IDs for healthcheck-derived backlog items (prevents duplicates)
- Respect autoImport flag in sync-backlog external tracker pull
- Await fetchIssues() calls for forward-compatible async adapter support
- Use source-to-prefix map for externalId instead of hardcoded GH/LIN
- Remove unused agentkitRoot param from backlog-viewer
- Use single timestamp for orchestrator items instead of three Date calls
- Add pnpm -- separator in CLI examples across templates and docs
- Fix hardcoded test count in intake flow documentation
- Clarify comma-separated --priority flag in plan document

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(intake): address review findings — regex, dedup, validation, tests

- Fix 7-column regex to require non-empty Source column, preventing
  greedy match on 6-column legacy rows (backlog-store)
- Escape pipe characters in markdown table cell values (backlog-store)
- Deduplicate manual items by title to prevent re-append on repeated
  imports (issue-normalizer)
- Add Array.isArray guard on gh CLI JSON output (github-adapter)
- Validate state/labels parameters and fix Windows timeout detection
  via err.signal (github-adapter)
- Forward since/limit flags to fetchIssues in sync-backlog-runner
- Warn on --direction push (unimplemented) and JSON-to-markdown fallback
- Add import-issues/backlog/sync-backlog to CLI showHelp() output
- Document all sync-backlog flags in template
- Add 39 new tests: github-adapter, tracker-adapter, backlog-viewer,
  sync-backlog-runner, plus expanded backlog-store and issue-normalizer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(plan): mark success criteria as completed

feat: Build full Homemade Intelligence platform (Phase 1-5)

- Backend: FastAPI + SQLite + ChromaDB + Anthropic 4-stage pipeline
  - Stage 1: semantic retrieval via ChromaDB (top-15 source chunks)
  - Stage 2: bias coverage enforcement + narrative divergence scoring
  - Stage 3: streaming English analysis with prompt cache_control blocks
  - Stage 4: concurrent TC general + elder formatting calls
  - Token tracker logs cost per stage with cache hit accounting
- Ingestion: RSS poller, GDELT, Yahoo Finance, FRED, manual text/URL inject
  - SHA-256 deduplication, APScheduler background jobs
- Predictions: CRUD + Brier score + calibration curve + skill score
- API: 14 endpoints across reports, predictions, ingestion, metrics routers
- Frontend: Next.js 16 App Router with SSE streaming, audience toggle
  - EN / 繁中 / 長輩版 tabs, bias score badge, reference odds bar
  - Recharts performance dashboard (Brier + calibration curve)
- Sources: rss_feeds.yaml with 25 feeds across 7 intelligence layers
- Tests: 31 passing (dedup, triangulation, Brier scoring, calibration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove llm_output relay, use sentinel_act as sole delivery mechanism (#63)

The llm_output relay was the pre-v0.8.0 delivery path that became redundant
after sentinel_act was introduced. It caused double-delivery and leaked
internal reasoning text (e.g. [[reply_to_current]]) to users.

- Remove handleLlmOutput(), llm_output hook, and sanitization functions
- Add fulfill() to HookResponseRelayManager, called on sentinel_act success
- Update prompt to instruct LLM that sentinel_act notify is the only delivery path
- Keep timeout fallback as safety net when LLM fails to act
